title: schizolegnia fraseri
intro: Schizolegnia fraseri, also known as Fraser's schizolegnia, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Achariaceae. It is a type of shrub or small tree that is native to Southeast Asia and is traditionally used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Schizolegnia fraseri include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever reduction, respiratory problems, and skin irritations.
